data.title: Xencor: Buy Rating Reaffirmed on Pipeline Momentum Despite Ultomiris Royalty Headwind
data.description:
William Blair analyst Matt Phipps has reiterated their bullish stance on XNCR stock, giving a Buy rating on February 26.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Matt Phipps has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors related to Xencor’s long‑term value drivers, despite a modest setback in royalty expectations from Ultomiris. While the limitation of AstraZeneca royalty payments to ex‑U.S. sales through 2028 slightly shortens the projected cash runway to mid‑2028, he views this as manageable in light of upcoming clinical milestones.

His positive stance is primarily anchored in the company’s advancing pipeline, with key readouts expected in 2026 from lead T‑cell engager programs XmAb819 in clear cell renal cell carcinoma and XmAb541 in select gynecologic and germ cell tumors. In addition, forthcoming Phase I results and preclinical data from the TL1A franchise, including XmAb942 and XmAb412, along with the ongoing Phase IIb XENITH‑UC trial, support the outlook for meaningful value creation beyond the near‑term royalty change.

Phipps covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Evommune, Inc., Argenx Se, and Janux Therapeutics Inc. According to TipRanks, Phipps has an average return of 4.1% and a 45.41% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released on February 26, Canaccord Genuity also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $38.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Okta: Broad-Based Outperformance and Conservative FY27 Outlook Skew Risk/Reward Toward Buy
data.description:
Shrenik Kothari, an analyst from Robert W. Baird, maintained the Buy rating on Okta. The associated price target remains the same with $125.00.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Shrenik Kothari has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors including Okta’s broad-based outperformance in FQ4 and its solid profitability profile. The company exceeded expectations on revenue, remaining performance obligations, operating margin, and EPS, while also posting a notably strong free cash flow margin, which reinforces confidence in the durability and quality of the business model.

He also views the FY27 outlook as prudently conservative, given that guidance already absorbs headwinds from shifting professional services to partners and from lower interest income tied to capital return actions. With double-digit growth in cRPO, healthy large-customer metrics, emerging AI and agentic identity tailwinds, and a $1B buyback supporting capital allocation, Kothari believes the risk/reward is skewed favorably and justifies a Buy recommendation.

In another report released yesterday, TipRanks – Google also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $82.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Balanced Risk-Reward Keeps Beazley at Hold Amid Weaker Premium Growth and Specialty Risks Reserve Strengthening
data.description:
Daniel Wilson-Omordia, an analyst from Morgan Stanley, maintained the Hold rating on Beazley. The associated price target is p1,335.00.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Daniel Wilson-Omordia has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors related to Beazley’s recent performance and outlook. The company’s full-year 2025 results revealed weaker-than-expected premium growth in the second half, with written premiums slightly below both market expectations and the prior year, and no forward guidance to support a stronger growth narrative.

While the overall combined ratio appeared broadly in line with consensus, this masked a notable underperformance in the Specialty Risks division, where substantial reserve strengthening tied in part to social inflation weighed on quality of earnings. At the same time, Beazley’s solvency ratio remains comfortably above management’s minimum target, and some lines such as Cyber, MAP and Property delivered better profitability, leading the analyst to view the risk‑reward as balanced rather than compellingly positive or negative at the current share price.

In another report released yesterday, RBC Capital also downgraded the stock to a Hold with a p1,300.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Deep Value Opportunity Amid Re-Accelerating Growth and Strengthening Profitability Supporting a Buy Rating and $7 Target
data.description:
Truist Financial analyst Terry Tillman has maintained their bullish stance on RSKD stock, giving a Buy rating today.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Terry Tillman has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors including accelerating growth, strengthening profitability, and an appealing valuation. He notes that gross merchandise volume and revenues are re-accelerating, with strong outperformance in gross margins, the company’s first-ever positive GAAP net income, and better-than-expected EBITDA and cash generation, all supported by a solid, debt-free balance sheet with substantial cash.

Tillman also emphasizes that new business signings and lead generation have improved markedly since the IPO, while Riskified’s technology and expanding product set, including opportunities in agentic commerce and fraud management for AI-driven merchant agents and LLM checkouts, position it well for future demand. Given these fundamentals, combined with the shares trading near 1x sales and roughly 8x 2027 EBITDA and supported by ongoing buybacks, he views the stock as a deep value opportunity with an attractive risk-reward profile, justifying his Buy rating and $7 price target despite recent sector-wide AI-related pressure on valuations.

Tillman covers the Technology sector, focusing on stocks such as Manhattan Associates, Shopify, and Klaviyo, Inc. Class A. According to TipRanks, Tillman has an average return of -7.5% and a 36.02% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released today, William Blair also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $4.67 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
